content.text: The producer lacks permissions on `/app` vhost. Let me check current permissions and the producer's configured user.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-user-permission-manage-3cda4425-cluster-a
echo "=== PERMISSIONS on /app ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app
echo "=== USERS ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_users
echo "=== POLICIES on /app ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p /app
echo "=== PRODUCER ENV/DEPLOY ==="
kubectl -n $NS get deploy app-producer -o yaml | grep -A 40 "containers:"

content.content:
=== PERMISSIONS on /app ===
Listing permissions for vhost "/app" ...
user	configure	write	read
admin	.*	.*	.*
app-user		.*	.*
=== USERS ===
Listing users ...
user	tags
admin	[administrator]
app-user	[management]
=== POLICIES on /app ===
Listing policies for vhost "/app" ...

=== PRODUCER ENV/DEPLOY ===
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          base="http://rabbitmq:15672/api"
          vhost="%2Fapp"
          queue="app-queue"
          auth="${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}"
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -s -u "$auth" "$base/vhosts" >/dev/null; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          payload='{"durable":true,"auto_delete":false,"arguments":{"x-queue-type":"classic","x-queue-mode":"lazy"}}'
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
            -H "content-type: application/json" \
            -XPUT "$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}" -d "$payload")
          if [ "$code" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)"
            exit 1
          fi
          i=0
          while true; do
            body='{"properties":{},"routing_key":"'"${queue}"'","payload":"msg-'"${i}"'","payload_encoding":"string"}'
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
              -H "content-type: application/json" \
              -XPOST "$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish" -d "$body")
            if [ "$code" -ne 200 ]; then
              echo "app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)"
              exit 1
            fi
            i=$((i+1))
            sleep 2
          done
        env:
        - name: APP_USER
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-user-permission-manage-3cda4425-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS get secret rabbitmq-app -o jsonpath='{.data.username}' | base64 -d; echo

content.text: Confirmed: `app-user` is missing the `configure` permission on `/app` vhost (empty regex means no access), which blocks queue declaration. I'll fix the permission.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-user-permission-manage-3cda4425-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app

content.text: Now let's restart the producer pod so it retries, and watch for it becoming Ready.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-user-permission-manage-3cda4425-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS delete pod -l app=app-producer
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deploy/app-producer --timeout=90s
